Home › Volusia County › Daytona Beach › CARRABBA'S ITALIAN GRILL
C 74

CARRABBA'S ITALIAN GRILL

This grade is calculated by us from Florida DBPR public inspection records — Florida does not issue official grades. How we calculate it →

Data last updated: March 20, 2026

CARRABBA'S ITALIAN GRILL is a Italian restaurant at 2200 W INTERNATIONAL SPEEDWAY BLVD in Daytona Beach, Volusia County, in Central Florida. At its most recent health inspection on March 20, 2026, it received a grade of C with a score of 74 out of 100. The inspection noted 2 high-priority, 1 intermediate, and 7 basic violations. By health score, it is safer than 18% of Volusia County restaurants. Diners give it an average rating of 4.5 stars across 3053 reviews. Typical prices fall in the $20–30 range. Menu options are noted as suitable for vegetarian, gluten-free, keto, paleo, and healthy diners.

Inspections trending: improving · last inspected 99 days ago

Get directions Call (386) 255-3344

Inspection history

The following records are from Florida DBPR public inspection records, most recent first.

  1. March 20, 2026 · Routine - Food

    Inspection Completed - No Further Action

    2 high-priority · 1 intermediate · 7 basic

    • 02 — Consumer advisory, date-marking & labeling of ready-to-eat foods
    • 03 — Temperature control of foods (cold/hot holding, cooking, cooling, reheating)
    • 08 — Cross-contamination: raw foods separated; food protected during prep/storage
    • 22 — Food-contact surfaces clean and sanitized (×3)
    • 23 — Nonfood-contact surfaces clean
    • 36 — Floors, walls, ceilings clean & maintained; proper ventilation (×3)
    • 47 — Electrical wiring/outlets in good repair (reporting only)

  2. September 15, 2025 · Routine - Food

    Call Back - Complied

    0 high-priority · 0 intermediate · 5 basic

    • 14 — Food-contact & nonfood-contact surfaces properly designed/maintained (×2)
    • 22 — Food-contact surfaces clean and sanitized
    • 23 — Nonfood-contact surfaces clean
    • 36 — Floors, walls, ceilings clean & maintained; proper ventilation

  3. September 12, 2025 · Routine - Food

    Warning Issued

    1 high-priority · 3 intermediate · 10 basic

    • 03 — Temperature control of foods (cold/hot holding, cooking, cooling, reheating)
    • 05 — Food & equipment thermometers provided and accurate
    • 06 — Potentially hazardous foods properly thawed
    • 14 — Food-contact & nonfood-contact surfaces properly designed/maintained (×2)
    • 22 — Food-contact surfaces clean and sanitized
    • 23 — Nonfood-contact surfaces clean
    • 24 — Storage & handling of clean equipment and utensils; air drying
    • 31 — Handwash sinks accessible; handwashing supplies & sign provided
    • 36 — Floors, walls, ceilings clean & maintained; proper ventilation (×4)
    • 53 — Food manager certification; employee food-safety training
